title: pauridia glabella var. glabella
intro: Pauridia glabella var. glabella, a species of flowering plant in the family Amaryllidaceae, is commonly known as the 'Yellow-Eyed Moonflower' due to its bright yellow flowers that bloom at night. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Pauridia glabella var. glabella include its potential to treat various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues. In herbal preparations, the plant's flowers and leaves are used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
